claims.claim: Prospeo states SOM benchmark is 1% of SAM in Year 1, 3% in Year 2, 5% in Year 3
claims.sourceTitle: Addressable Markets: TAM, SAM & SOM Guide (2026)
claims.excerpt: SOM benchmarks that won't get you laughed out of the room: 1% of SAM in Year 1, 3% in Year 2, 5% in Year 3.

claims.claim: Prospeo elsewhere frames realistic SOM as 1-5% of SAM over a 36-month (three-year) window, not as a Year-1-only figure
claims.sourceTitle: TAM vs SAM vs SOM: Definitions, Formulas & 2026 Guide
claims.excerpt: What's a realistic SOM? 1-5% of SAM over 36 months for early-stage companies. If you're claiming 10%+, investors will push back.

claims.claim: Prospeo's own worked example applies a 1% Year 1 capture rate to derive SOM from SAM
claims.sourceTitle: TAM SAM SOM Example: Real Numbers for 2026
claims.excerpt: Apply a 1% Year 1 capture rate and your SOM is ~$2.7M.

claims.claim: IdeaPlan states SOM is typically 1-5% of SAM for early-stage companies, without specifying a year
claims.excerpt: For early-stage companies, SOM is typically 1-5% of SAM. For growing companies with product-market fit, SOM can reach 10-20% of SAM.

claims.claim: Tractian's revenue grew from $200K to $32M over four years, implying a starting-point figure far below the $1.2M cited elsewhere
claims.sourceTitle: Tractian: How Two Sons of Factory Engineers Built a $32M Business on Sensors That Predict Breakdowns — Solopreneur
claims.excerpt: The company grew from $200K to $32M in revenue over four years and serves more than 1,000 plants, including John Deere, Caterpillar, and Goodyear.

claims.claim: Tractian reported $1.2M in ARR after a stated 10x growth event, with a $3.7M funding round at a $15M valuation
claims.sourceTitle: LATAM SaaS + IoT Grows 10x to $1.2m in ARR, $3.7m Round at $15m Valuation
claims.excerpt: TRACTIAN Grows 10x to $1.2m in ARR, $3.7m Round at $15m Valuation

claims.claim: Predictive maintenance market (Tractian's comparable SAM/TAM category) is sized at $14.9B by 2026 per MarketsandMarkets
claims.sourceTitle: Tractian: How Two Sons of Factory Engineers Built a $32M Business on Sensors That Predict Breakdowns — Solopreneur
claims.excerpt: Market $14.9 млрд к 2026 году (предиктивное обслуживание, по данным MarketsandMarkets)

claims.claim: Tractian has raised $200 million in total funding and serves more than 1,500 manufacturers
claims.sourceTitle: Tractian Funding | $32.2M Revenue | Complete Analysis | Extruct AI
claims.excerpt: The company says it serves more than 1,500 manufacturers and has raised $200 million in total funding.

claims.claim: Samsara reported $1.9B in FY26 ARR with 30% YoY growth, illustrating a mature-stage obtainable-share trajectory unlike an early-stage Year-1 SOM
claims.sourceTitle: Samsara's FY26 Shows Accelerated Growth at Scale as AI Platform Delivers Clear Benefits to Organizations Powering the Global Economy
claims.excerpt: $1.9B in FY26 ARR1, 30% YoY growth, representing a year of durable and efficient growth

claims.claim: Prospeo's SOM guide reports investors spend an average of 3 minutes 44 seconds on an entire pitch deck, underscoring benchmark figures are meant as quick heuristics rather than precise year-specific definitions
claims.sourceTitle: Addressable Markets: TAM, SAM & SOM Guide (2026)
claims.excerpt: Investors spend 3 minutes and 44 seconds on your entire pitch deck.

tensions.text: Prospeo publishes two inconsistent SOM framings on its own site: one page gives staged annual benchmarks (1% Year 1, 3% Year 2, 5% Year 3, summing to 9% cumulative by Year 3), while another page frames the realistic SOM as a flat '1-5% of SAM over 36 months' — i.e., a single three-year aggregate range rather than a sequential per-year build. The obtainableFactor node's derivation treats 1% as a clean, isolated Year-1 figure, but the source itself is not internally consistent about whether these percentages are annual snapshots or cumulative three-year ranges.

The obtainableFactor derivation cites 'the Tractian comparable (first-year ~$1.2M against a multi-$B market)' as supporting evidence that ≤1% is plausible, but the sourced Tractian data shows revenue starting at $200K (not $1.2M) and reaching $1.2M in ARR only after a reported '10x' growth event in a 2022 funding article — meaning $1.2M was not clearly a first-year figure, undermining the specific number used in the cross-check.

absences: No first-year (or any-year) revenue figures were found for data-center-specific hardware entrants (e.g., rack-PDU or DC electrical gear vendors) — the only comparables returned (Tractian, Samsara, Augury) are general industrial IoT/fleet-management companies, not the intended DC hardware category.

No quantified revenue or ARR figures were found for Augury despite it being surfaced as a candidate comparable — its page contains only qualitative claims ('1.1B+ hours of real machine readings') with no dollar-denominated first-year or obtainable-share data.

The node derivation's cross-check claims about 'early-procurement mechanism (DC electrical gear locked 12-24mo before fit-out)' and 'open 2026 EU rack-PDU tenders' were not corroborated or found anywhere in the returned search results — these specific supporting facts remain unsourced from this query pass.

No source was found stating Tractian's or any comparable company's SOM as an explicit percentage of its own stated SAM/TAM in Year 1, which is the specific benchmark needed to validate the 1% figure against a real-world outcome rather than a stated aspiration.
